children.heading: Establishment; merit and other employment requirements
children.label: a Establishment; merit and other employment requirements

children.text: be established in accordance with and be subject to the provisions of the Panama Canal Treaty of 1977 and related agreements, the provisions of this part, and any other applicable provision of law;
be based on the consideration of the merit of each employee or candidate for employment and the qualifications and fitness of the employee to hold the position concerned;
conform, to the extent practicable and consistent with the provisions of this chapter, to the policies, principles, and standards applicable to the competitive service;

in the case of employees who are citizens of the United States, provide for the appropriate interchange of those employees between positions under the Panama Canal Employment System and positions in the competitive service; and
not be subject to the provisions of title 5, unless specifically made applicable by this chapter.

children.heading: Applicability to any Executive agency and Smithsonian Institution
children.label: b Applicability to any Executive agency and Smithsonian Institution

children.text: The head of any Executive agency (other than the Commission) and the Smithsonian Institution may elect to have the Panama Canal Employment System made applicable in whole or in part to personnel of that agency in the Republic of Panama.

Any Executive agency (other than the Commission) and the Smithsonian Institution, to the extent of any election under paragraph (1), shall conduct its employment and pay practices relating to employees in accordance with the Panama Canal Employment System.

Notwithstanding any other provision of this chapter, the Panama Canal Act Amendments of 1996 (subtitle B of title XXXV of ; ), or the Panama Canal Transition Facilitation Act of 1997 (subtitle B of title XXXV of ; 110 Stat. 2062), or the Panama Canal Commission Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 1999, this subpart, as in effect on , shall continue to apply to an Executive agency or the Smithsonian Institution to the extent of an election under paragraph (1) by the head of the agency or the Institution, respectively.Public Law 104–201110 Stat. 2860Public Law 105–851So in original. Probably should be “111”.1September 22, 1996

children.heading: Exclusion of employees or positions from coverage
children.label: c Exclusion of employees or positions from coverage
children.text: The Commission may exclude any employee or position from coverage under any provision of this subpart, other than the interchange rights extended under subsection (a)(4).
